Again He Proves from the Gospel that Christ is God.

ccel.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

title: Again He Proves from the Gospel that Christ is God.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Argument.—Again He Proves from the Gospel that Christ is God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: If Christ is only man, how is it that He says, “Though I bear record of myself, yet my record is true: because I know whence I came, and whither I go; ye know not whence I came, and whither I go.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Ye judge after the flesh?”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Behold, also He says, that He shall return thither whence He bears witness that He came before, as being sent,—to wit, from heaven.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: He came down therefore from whence He came, in the same manner as He goes thither from whence He descended.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Whence if Christ were only man, He would not have come thence, and therefore would not depart thither, because He would not have come thence.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Moreover, by coming thence, whence as man He could not have come, He shows Himself to have come as God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For the Jews, ignorant and untaught in the matter of this very descent of His, made these heretics their successors, seeing that to them it is said, “Ye know not whence I come, and whither I go: ye judge after the flesh.”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: As much they as the Jews, holding that the carnal birth of Christ was the only one, believed that Christ was nothing else than man; not considering this point, that as man could not come from heaven, so as that he might return thither, He who descended thence must be God, seeing that man could not come thence.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: If Christ is only man, how does He say, “Ye are from below, I am from above; ye are of this world, I am not of this world?”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But therefore if every man is of this world, and Christ is for that reason in this world, is He only man?

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But consider what He says: “I am not of this world.”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Does He then speak falsely when He says “of this world,” if He is only man?

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Or if He does not speak falsely, He is not of this world; He is therefore not man only, because He is not of this world.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But that it should not be a secret who He was, He declared whence He was: “I,” said He, “am from above,” that is, from heaven, whence man cannot come, for he was not made in heaven.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: He is God, therefore, who is from above, and therefore He is not of this world; although, moreover, in a certain manner He is of this world: wherefore Christ is not God only, but man also.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: As reasonably in the way in which He is not of this world according to the divinity of the Word, so He is of this world according to the frailty of the body that He has taken upon Him.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For man is joined with God, and God is linked with man.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But on that account this Christ here laid more stress on the one aspect of His sole divinity, because the Jewish blindness contemplated in Christ the aspect alone of the flesh; and thence in the present passage He passed over in silence the frailty of the body, which is of the world, and spoke of His divinity alone, which is not of the world: so that in proportion as they had inclined to believe Him to be only man, in that proportion Christ might draw them to consider His divinity, so as to believe Him to be God, desirous to overcome their incredulity concerning His divinity by omitting in the meantime any mention of His human condition, and by setting before them His divinity alone.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: If Christ is man only, how does He say, “I proceeded forth and came from God,” when it is evident that man was made by God, and did not proceed forth from Him?

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But in the way in which as man He proceeded not from God, thus the Word of God proceeded, of whom it is said, “My heart hath uttered forth a good Word;” which, because it is from God, is with reason also with God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And this, too, since it was not uttered without effect, reasonably makes all things: “For all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But this Word whereby all things were made (is God).

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: “And God,” says he, “was the Word.”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Therefore God proceeded from God, in that the Word which proceeded is God, who proceeded forth from God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: If Christ is only man, how does He say, “If any man shall keep my word, he shall not see death for ever?”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Not to see death for ever! what is this but immortality?

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But immortality is the associate of divinity, because both the divinity is immortal, and immortality is the fruit of divinity.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For every man is mortal; and immortality cannot be from that which is mortal.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Therefore from Christ, as a mortal man, immortality cannot arise.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: “But,” says He, “whosoever keepeth my word, shall not see death for ever;” therefore the word of Christ affords immortality, and by immortality affords divinity.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But although it is not possible to maintain that one who is himself mortal can make another immortal, yet this word of Christ not only sets forth, but affords immortality: certainly He is not man only who gives immortality, which if He were only man He could not give; but by giving divinity by immortality, He proves Himself to be God by offering divinity, which if He were not God He could not give.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: If Christ was only man, how did He say, “Before Abraham was, I Am?”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For no man can be before Him from whom he himself is; nor can it be that any one should have been prior to him of whom he himself has taken his origin.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And yet Christ, although He is born of Abraham, says that He is before Abraham.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Either, therefore, He says what is not true, and deceives, if He was not before Abraham, seeing that He was of Abraham; or He does not deceive, if He is also God, and was before Abraham.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And if this were not so, it follows that, being of Abraham, He could not be before Abraham.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: If Christ was only man, how does He say, “And I know them, and my sheep follow me; and I give unto them eternal life, and they shall never perish?”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And yet, since every man is bound by the laws of mortality, and therefore is unable to keep himself for ever, much more will he be unable to keep another for ever.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But Christ promises to give salvation for ever, which if He does not give, He is a deceiver; if He gives, He is God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: But He does not deceive, for He gives what He promises.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Therefore He is God who proffers eternal salvation, which man, being unable to keep himself for ever, cannot be able to give to another.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: If Christ is only man, what is that which He says, “I and the Father are one?”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For how can it be that “I and the Father are one,” if He is not both God and the Son?—who may therefore be called one, seeing that He is of Himself, being both His Son, and being born of Him, being declared to have proceeded from Him, by which He is also God; which when the Jews thought to be hateful, and believed to be blasphemous, for that He had shown Himself in these discourses to be God, and therefore rushed at once to stoning, and set to work passionately to hurl stones, He strongly refuted His adversaries by the example and witness of the Scriptures.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: “If,” said He, “He called them gods to whom the words of God were given, and the Scriptures cannot be broken, ye say of Him whom the Father sanctified, and sent into this world, Thou blasphemest, because I said, I am the Son of God.”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: By which words He did not deny Himself to be God, but rather He confirmed the assertion that He was God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For because, undoubtedly, they are said to be gods unto whom the words of God were given, much more is He God who is found to be superior to all these.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And nevertheless He refuted the calumny of blasphemy in a fitting manner with lawful tact.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: For He wishes that He should be thus understood to be God, as the Son of God, and He would not wish to be understood to be the Father Himself.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Thus He said that He was sent, and showed them that He had manifested many good works from the Father; whence He desired that He should not be understood to be the Father, but the Son.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: And in the latter portion of His defence He made mention of the Son, not the Father, when He said, “Ye say, Thou blasphemest, because I said, I am the Son of God.”

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: Thus, as far as pertains to the guilt of blasphemy, He calls Himself the Son, not the Father; but as pertaining to His divinity, by saying, “I and the Father are one,” He proved that He was the Son of God.

verses.book: A Treatise of Novatian Concerning the Trinity.
verses.text: He is God, therefore, but God in such a manner as to be the Son, not the Father.
